backfill_note: This run was executed on 2026-06-26; its raw artifacts (the standalone deno.exe, both node_modules trees, the DENO_DIR/npm caches) were discarded per the finite-disk policy and predate this repo. run.sh + fixture/package.json are reconstructed from the run's recorded methodology so a third party can reproduce the METHOD. results.json holds the numbers measured on 2026-06-26 — re-running today will drift: install timings are machine/cache-dependent, and audit counts move as the advisory DB updates. No artifact hashes were captured at the time, so none are invented here.

environment.note: Install timing is machine- and cache-dependent; the ratio (deno install vs npm install) and the qualitative behaviours are the portable signals, not absolute milliseconds.

subject.name: deno
subject.install: GitHub release v2.8.0 (Windows zip), invoked as a separate binary (nothing global changed)
subject.note: Pin 2.8.x in the post. Deno 2.8.0 = 2026-05-22; Deno 2.9.0 shipped 2026-06-25 and changes lockfile seeding (it can seed deno.lock from package-lock/pnpm-lock/yarn.lock/bun.lock), so 2.9 behaviour would not match this run.

baseline.name: npm
baseline.invocation: npm install / npm audit / npm ci / npm pack

data_source.name: GitHub Advisory Database (empirical)
data_source.naming_caveat: npm audit and deno audit both emitted the same GHSA-* IDs, so empirically they hit the same GitHub Advisory data. We do NOT claim the source is OSV: Deno's own docs name it inconsistently (GitHub CVE / vulnerability databases / npm advisory). The only empirical claim is 'the same GHSA IDs showed up in both'.
data_source.drift_warning: Vulnerability COUNTS in results.json are as-of 2026-06-26 and will change as the advisory DB adds/updates advisories. The fixture, commands, layout/phantom-dep/pack/parity behaviours, and the install-speed ratio remain reproducible; exact audit counts are a point-in-time snapshot.

raw_artifacts.retention_policy: discarded at run time (finite local disk)
raw_artifacts.note: The downloaded deno 2.8.0 binary, both fixture node_modules trees, and the caches were deleted after the run. No hashes were captured at the time (this predates the benchmarks repo). The one hash the run did record — a 12-hex prefix for the identical node/deno program output — is carried through verbatim as a prefix in results.json, not padded into a fabricated full digest. The reconstructed harness is committed instead of fake artifacts.
